week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 9-category week, league-best offensive and pitching balance, clear Week 1 statement
2. BaseballPro77 | 8–2 win backed by league-high 8 HR, Trout and Murakami both erupted, deep and dangerous roster
3. North Correa | 7–3 win with a league-best 23 runs scored and solid pitching ERA of 2.73, Arozarena and Bregman clicked early
4. Benchwarmers | 7–3 win built on Zach Neto's explosive week (6 R, 2 HR) and strong RBI production across the lineup
5. General Hospital | Tied but led the league in RBI (23) and posted the highest team OBP (.414) with a dominant 3-QS pitching week — too talented to rank lower
6. Albuquerque Bombers | 6–4 win was somewhat fortunate but Yelich, Turang, and Lile all contributed; pitching ERA of 2.48 was legitimately excellent
7. Athletic Park Dragons | 5–4 squeaker win; Sánchez's 0.50 WHIP gem and Giménez's clutch RBI day saved them, but offense needs more punch
8. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Tied despite 7 HR and solid pitching; Donovan was a multi-cat star and this roster has clear upside heading into Week 2
9. Hiroshima Radiation | Lost 5–4 in a tight one with a strong 2.35 ERA and 44 K from the staff — bad luck more than bad roster
10. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 7–3 but Bryan Woo and Juan Soto offer real upside; pitching ERA of 5.79 must improve
11. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 6–4 despite 6 HR and 5 SB — the 6.00 ERA and 0 QS from pitching is a glaring problem to address immediately
12. Riverside Roughnecks | 9–1 blowout loss and a catastrophic 7.91 ERA with 0 QS; Langeliers and Lewis hit well but pitching is broken
13. Canberra Wombats | Lost 8–2 with 0 HR and 0 QS; only Dylan Cease showed up on the mound and the offense had no power whatsoever
14. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7–3 despite elite pitching metrics in spots; 6.02 ERA and .271 OBP are deeply problematic and the roster balance is badly off
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8–2 week, elite 2.11 ERA, Max Muncy launching HR, deepest all-around roster through 3 weeks | up
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood and Contreras were unstoppable; 40 R and .412 OBP is a league statement | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5-HR explosion anchored a solid 5–3 win; consistent across all three weeks | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Crushed Canberra 8–2 with 64 hits and 9 SB; Bryce Harper's .640 OBP a weapon | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6–3 but posted a 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP — pitching is elite; offense needs to wake up | down
6. North Correa | Lost the matchup but 11 SB and Ohtani's 3 HR show real firepower; speed category dominance is a threat | same
7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8–2 but pitching was solid with 75 Ks and 3.99 ERA; hitting depth is the concern | down
8. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is must-start elite, but tying General Hospital when you should've won is a missed opportunity | same
9. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Pitching is genuinely strong — 1.13 WHIP, 7 NSVH — but hitting is a serious structural problem | up
10. General Hospital | Kumar Rocker add is intriguing; 70 Ks in a tie stings, but the pitching foundation is real | down
11. Albuquerque Bombers | Lost 5–4 in a close one; Brice Turang's .474 OBP is a bright spot but pitching remains shaky at 5.05 ERA | same
12. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 4 HR and they still got steamrolled 8–2; pitching is simply not good enough | down
13. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 on the back of Trevor Story's 9 RBI but 0 quality starts and a 5.04 ERA is unsustainable | same
14. Benchwarmers | The 6–3 win flatters a team with a 5.53 ERA and 34 Ks; luck index confirms they should be much lower | same
